zh.lifeSummary: Juan Nuñez I "el Gordo" de Lara是Nuño Gonzalez de Lara El Bueno y Diaz de Haro和Teresa Alfonso de León的兒子，出生。他於1260年之前與Teresa Alvarez de Azagra在西班牙安達盧希亞科爾多瓦蒙塔爾巴 德 科爾多瓦拉臘 德 洛斯 音法斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他於1292年被列為adelantado mayor de la frontera de andalucía。他去世於1294年4月在西班牙安達盧希亞科爾多瓦，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Juan Nuñez I "el Gordo" de Lara是Nuño Gonzalez de Lara El Bueno y Diaz de Haro和Teresa Alfonso de León的儿子，出生。他于1260年之前与Teresa Alvarez de Azagra在西班牙安達盧希亞科爾多瓦蒙塔爾巴 德 科爾多瓦拉臘 德 洛斯 音法斯结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他于1292年被列为adelantado mayor de la frontera de andalucía。他去世于1294年4月在西班牙安達盧希亞科爾多瓦，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lara
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Jorge, Luis, Pedro, Jesus, Roberto, Rafael, Mario.
Spanish: habitational name from a place called Lara de los Infantes in Burgos province. Compare   De Lara  .

variants.name: De Lara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Juan, Jose, Luis, Mauricio, Pedro, Alfonso, Ana, Jaime, Javier, Jorge, Merlinda.
Spanish: variant, with the preposition de ‘from’, of Lara .

nameMeanings.name: Juan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , now also used as a given name in English-speaking countries, in spite of the unfavourable associations with Don Juan, the heartless seducer of  Mozart's  opera Don Giovanni (  1788 ) and libertine hero of  Byron's  satirical epic (  1819–24 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: Nuñez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Francisco, Miguel, Rafael, Pedro, Jorge, Ramon.  Portuguese Guilherme, Joaquim, Vasco, Wenceslao.
Spanish (Núñez): patronymic from the medieval personal name Nuño (see Nuno ).

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the name in 1 above at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Nuno
meanings: Anglicized form of the Irish name Úna , assimilated to the name of the Roman goddess Juno, consort of Jupiter.

nameMeanings.name: "el
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: Gordo"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Transferred use of the Scottish surname, which is derived from a place name. It is a matter of dispute whether it referred originally to the Gordon in Berwickshire or to a similarly named place in Normandy. As a given name it seems to have been taken up in honour of  Charles George Gordon  (  1833–85 ), the British general who died at Khartoum.

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: Juan Nuñez I "el Gordo" de Lara was born as the son of Nuño Gonzalez de Lara El Bueno y Diaz de Haro and Teresa Alfonso de León. He married Teresa Alvarez de Azagra before 1260, in Lara de los Infantes, Montalbán de Córdoba, Córdoba, Andalusia, Spain. They were the parents of at least 1 son. In 1292, his occupation is listed as adelantado mayor de la frontera de andalucía. He died in April 1294, in Córdoba, Andalusia, Spain, and was buried in Burgos, Castilla y León, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Juan Nuñez I "el Gordo" de Lara est né, fils de Nuño Gonzalez de Lara El Bueno y Diaz de Haro et Teresa Alfonso de León. Il a épousé Teresa Alvarez de Azagra avant 1260, à Lara de los Infantes, Montalbán de Córdoba, Córdoba, Andalucía, Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. En 1292, sa profession de son indiquée est adelantado mayor de la frontera de andalucía. Il est décédé en avril 1294, à Córdoba, Andalucía, Espagne, et a été enterré à Burgos, Castilla y León, Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Juan Nuñez I "el Gordo" de Lara wurde, als Sohn von Nuño Gonzalez de Lara El Bueno y Diaz de Haro und Teresa Alfonso de León geboren. Er hat vor 1260 in Lara de los Infantes, Montalbán de Córdoba, Córdoba, Andalucía, Spanien Teresa Alvarez de Azagra geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. 1292 ist sein Beruf als adelantado mayor de la frontera de andalucía aufgeführt. Er starb im April 1294 in Córdoba, Andalucía, Spanien und wurde in Burgos, Castilla y León, Spanien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Juan Nuñez I "el Gordo" de Lara è nato il come figlio di Nuño Gonzalez de Lara El Bueno y Diaz de Haro e Teresa Alfonso de León. Lui sposato Teresa Alvarez de Azagra prima del 1260, nel Lara de los Infantes, Montalbán de Córdoba, Córdoba, Andalucía, Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Nel 1292, la sua occupazione è indicata come adelantado mayor de la frontera de andalucía. Morì nel aprile 1294, nel Córdoba, Andalucía, Spagna, e fu sepolto a Burgos, Castilla y León, Spagna.

ja.lifeSummary: Juan Nuñez I "el Gordo" de LaraはNuño Gonzalez de Lara El Bueno y Diaz de HaroとTeresa Alfonso de Leónの息子として生まれた。1260年以前にスペインアンダルクiアクoルドバモンタルブaン ド クoルドバラーア ド ロス インファンツでTeresa Alvarez de Azagraと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。1292年，職業はadelantado mayor de la frontera de andalucíaと記録されている。彼は1294年4月にスペインアンダルクiアクoルドバで死去し，スペインカスティラ イ レoンブルゴスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Juan Nuñez I "el Gordo" de Lara(은)는 Nuño Gonzalez de Lara El Bueno y Diaz de Haro(와)과 Teresa Alfonso de León의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1260년 이전 스페인 앤다루ci아 co르도바 몽알브a 데 co르도바 라라 데 로스 인팡츠에서 Teresa Alvarez de Azagra(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 1292년, 그의 직업은 adelantado mayor de la frontera de andalucía이었다. 그는 1294년 4월 스페인 앤다루ci아 co르도바에서 사망했으며 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 버고스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Juan Nuñez I "el Gordo" de Lara nasceu filho de Nuño Gonzalez de Lara El Bueno y Diaz de Haro e Teresa Alfonso de León. Ele casou-se com Teresa Alvarez de Azagra antes 1260, em Lara de los Infantes, Montalbán de Córdoba, Córdoba, Andaluzia, Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Em 1292, sua profissão aparece como adelantado mayor de la frontera de andalucía. Ele faleceu em abril de 1294, em Córdoba, Andaluzia, Espanha, e foi sepultado em Burgos, Castilla y León, Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Juan Nuñez I "el Gordo" de Lara родился. Его родителей звали Nuño Gonzalez de Lara El Bueno y Diaz de Haro и Teresa Alfonso de León. Он женился на женщине по имени Teresa Alvarez de Azagra перед 1260 г. в местности лара де лос инфантесь, монталбaн де кoрдоба, кoрдоба, андалукiа, Испания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. В 1292 году он работал по профессии adelantado mayor de la frontera de andalucía. Он умер апрель 1294 г. в местности кoрдоба, андалукiа, Испания и был похоронен в местности бургос, кастилла и леoн, Испания.

es.lifeSummary: Juan Nuñez I "el Gordo" de Lara nació como hijo de Nuño Gonzalez de Lara El Bueno y Diaz de Haro y Teresa Alfonso de León. Se casó con Teresa Alvarez de Azagra antes de 1260, en Lara de los Infantes, Montalbán de Córdoba, Córdoba, Andalucía, España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. En 1292, su ocupación registrada era adelantado mayor de la frontera de andalucía. Murió en abril de 1294, en Córdoba, Andalucía, España, y fue sepultado en Burgos, Castilla y León, España.
